skill.name: swarm-planner
skill.description: [EXPLICIT INVOCATION ONLY] Creates dependency-aware implementation plans optimized for parallel multi-agent execution.
skill.summary: Creates dependency-aware implementation plans with explicit task dependencies for parallel multi-agent execution
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 18 patterns (12 external_commands, 6 weak_crypto). Manual review confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES: backticks are markdown syntax for example task IDs (T1, depends_on: []), and 'description' is a documentation field, not cryptographic code. No shell execution, no crypto algorithms, no security risks present.

content.value_statement: This skill helps AI agents create detailed implementation plans with explicit task dependencies that enable safe parallel execution across multiple agents.
actual_capabilities: Analyze codebase architecture and dependencies before planning
Create implementation plans with explicit task dependencies
Generate parallel execution groups based on dependency chains
Use Context7 for accurate external library documentation
Spawn subagents to review plans for gaps and issues
Structure plans for maximum parallelization opportunities
limitations: Does not execute tasks - only creates plans
Does not handle runtime task execution or monitoring
Context7 or web search required for external dependencies

use_cases.title: Complex Feature Implementation
use_cases.description: Break down a complex feature into atomic, parallelizable tasks with clear dependencies for team execution
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working on multi-file features

use_cases.title: Large-Scale Refactoring
use_cases.description: Plan refactoring efforts that can safely run in parallel across different code areas
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams doing major code reorganizations

use_cases.title: New Project Setup
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive implementation plans for new projects with proper task ordering

prompt_templates.title: Basic Planning Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the swarm-planner skill to create an implementation plan for [describe your task]. Focus on identifying all dependencies and which tasks can run in parallel.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple implementation plan with dependency tracking

prompt_templates.title: Complex Feature Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use swarm-planner to analyze our codebase and create a detailed implementation plan for adding [feature description]. Include architecture investigation, external library research via Context7, and explicit dependency mapping.
prompt_templates.scenario: For complex features requiring codebase analysis and external dependencies

prompt_templates.title: Refactoring Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use swarm-planner to create a refactoring plan for [what to refactor]. Identify which files depend on each other, create parallel execution groups, and specify validation steps.
prompt_templates.description: Planning large-scale refactoring with dependency awareness

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Agent Coordination
prompt_templates.prompt: Use swarm-planner to create an implementation plan that assigns specific tasks to different agents. Ensure each task has clear depends_on fields so agents can execute in parallel where possible.
prompt_templates.scenario: When coordinating multiple AI agents on a single project

output_examples.input: Use swarm-planner to create a plan for adding user authentication to our React app
output_examples.output: Plan saved to authentication-plan.md with tasks: T1 Create auth schema (no deps), T2 Install auth packages (no deps), T3 Create auth service (depends_on T1), T4 Add login UI (depends_on T2,T3), T5 Write tests (depends_on T4)

output_examples.input: Plan a database migration with parallel schema and data tasks
output_examples.output: T1: Create migration files (no deps), T2: Backup data (no deps), T3: Apply schema (depends_on T1), T4: Migrate data (depends_on T2,T3), T5: Verify (depends_on T4)

best_practices: Always use Context7 to fetch current documentation for external libraries before finalizing tasks
Stop and ask clarifying questions when architecture is unclear rather than making assumptions
Ensure every task has explicit depends_on field, even if empty
Review generated plans with a subagent to catch missing dependencies
anti_patterns: Creating tasks without explicit dependency declarations
Assuming task order without documenting dependencies
Implementing tasks instead of just planning them

faq.question: How do I invoke this skill?
faq.answer: This skill requires explicit invocation. You must explicitly call it rather than having it trigger automatically.

faq.question: What does dependency-aware mean?
faq.answer: Each task explicitly declares which other tasks must complete before it can start using the depends_on field.

faq.question: Can tasks run in parallel?
faq.answer: Yes. Tasks with empty or satisfied dependencies can be executed in parallel by different agents simultaneously.

faq.question: Do I need Context7?
faq.answer: Context7 is required for accurate external library documentation. If unavailable, use web search instead.

faq.question: Does this skill execute the plan?
faq.answer: No. This skill only creates the implementation plan. It does not execute any tasks.

faq.question: How does subagent review work?
faq.answer: After saving the plan, spawn a subagent to review it for missing dependencies, ordering issues, gaps, and edge cases.
